Title: Microbiota-mediated nitrogen fixation and microhabitat homeostasis in aerial root-mucilage

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Diazotrophic bacteria isolated from Heterotis rotundifolia aerial root mucilage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nitrogen nutrition, chlorophyll labeling, biomass accumulation and growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of nifH-bearing mucilage microbiota supports atmospheric nitrogen fixation that contributes substantially to plant nitrogen acquisition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aerial root mucilage microhabitat; 15N2 feeding; aerial root removal or mucilage removal
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected as cloned alleles from cultured diazotrophs able to grow on nitrogen-free medium
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Different nifH alleles were cloned from mucilage-associated diazotrophic isolates. 2. Most nifH-containing isolates showed nitrogen fixation activity in 15N2-labeling experiments. 3. Intact aerial root mucilage microbiota fixed atmospheric nitrogen and supported host plant growth.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Aerial root mucilage microhabitat; incorporated into aerial roots, stems and leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a microbiome functional marker for selecting or engineering nitrogen-fixing mucilage-associated consortia to improve plant nitrogen use efficiency
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing; metabolomics; RNA-seq; genome sequencing; NGDC BioProject PRJCA009607
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the only explicitly named metabolism-relevant gene in the Results. It is microbial, not plant-derived, and functions as a marker of diazotrophic capacity rather than a host plant gene.
